appliedUpdates.description: Added note about russh ChannelStream stderr limitation: ChannelStream only reads stdout (ChannelMsg::Data) on its AsyncRead side; stderr (ExtendedData ext=1) is handled internally by russh and not accessible through the stream's read side.

Documented type compatibility contract: exec_stream() returns Pin<Box<dyn AsyncReadWrite>> which is directly compatible with all transport constructors accepting T: AsyncRead + AsyncWrite + Send + 'static. Works via Deref on Pin<Box<dyn X>>.

Documented initialization pattern difference: PiAcpTransport uses explicit handshake() method while DroidAcpTransport uses background task polling (wait_for system/init). Factory needs different init code paths for each.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note about SSH handle lock management convention: the preferred pattern is to drop the handle lock immediately after opening a channel (as done in exec_inner() and exec_stream()), not hold it through into_stream() (as in the older open_streamlocal()). New SSH methods should follow the early-drop pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: ssh-exec-stream review found open_streamlocal() holds the lock longer than necessary, while the newer exec_stream() correctly drops early. Pre-existing inconsistency across ssh.rs.

rejectedObservations.observation: Factory takes &Arc<SshClient> rather than &SshClient — slightly unconventional for Rust.
rejectedObservations.reason: This matches the existing pattern throughout the codebase (ssh_bootstrap functions also use &Arc<SshClient>). Not new information, and not a problem worth documenting.

rejectedObservations.observation: Double timeout in create_droid_acp() — outer tokio::time::timeout wraps inner polling loop that already times out.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a code quality issue in the implementation, not a knowledge artifact. Already captured as a non-blocking issue in the provider-factory review. Not appropriate for library/services.yaml.
